steps.title: Turn the min and max into bounds on $s$
steps.content: The minimum recorded rate was $0.6$ in/hr, so every recorded $s$ satisfies $s\ge 0.6$. The maximum recorded rate was $1.8$ in/hr, so every recorded $s$ also satisfies $s\le 1.8$.

steps.title: Combine into one inequality
steps.content: Both conditions must hold simultaneously, giving the compound inequality$$0.6\le s\le 1.8.$$

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• A) $s\ge 2.4$: the maximum recorded was $1.8$, so values $\ge 2.4$ were never reached. ✗
  • B) $s\ge 1.8$: this allows $s$ above $1.8$ and excludes $s$ between $0.6$ and $1.8$, which is wrong on both ends. ✗
  • C) $0\le s\le 0.6$: this caps $s$ at $0.6$, but rates up to $1.8$ were recorded. ✗
  • D) $0.6\le s\le 1.8$: matches the recorded minimum and maximum exactly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
